title: The Docket - Week 4
subject: The contract AI that hallucinated a Supreme Court case — and the lawyer who cited it anyway
preheader: Also: why Microsoft's new legal copilot changes everything for small firms
bodyHtml:
The Docket

LegalTech AI Hub · Week of April 22, 2026

You’d think we’d have learned from the Mata v. Avianca debacle in 2023. Apparently not. Here’s your weekly briefing.

🚨 Another Hallucinated Citation Makes It to Court

A Texas personal injury attorney filed a motion citing Henderson v. National Transport Corp. — a case that doesn’t exist. The AI tool he used generated a plausible-looking citation, complete with a fabricated procedural history. The judge sanctioned the attorney $5,000 and referred the matter to the state bar. The attorney’s defense: “The software said it verified the citation.” The software’s documentation says it doesn’t. Always verify. Read our guide on AI verification workflows.

💻 Microsoft Copilot Gets Legal-Specific Features

Microsoft announced legal-domain features for Copilot in Word and Outlook: clause comparison in tracked changes, automated engagement letter drafting, and matter-organized email threads. For the 80% of small firms already using Microsoft 365, this is AI legal tech that requires zero new procurement. That’s a distribution advantage nobody else can match.

🌐 Canada Releases AI Ethics Framework for Legal Profession

The Federation of Law Societies of Canada published a 40-page framework on responsible AI use in legal practice. It’s the most comprehensive professional guidance document any bar association has produced. Key takeaway: competence in AI supervision is now a professional obligation, not a nice-to-have. Check our glossary for key AI terms every lawyer should know.

Tool Spotlight

Legal AI Readiness Quiz

Not sure if your firm is ready for AI? Take our 5-minute readiness assessment. You’ll get a personalized score across five dimensions: data infrastructure, workflow automation potential, team readiness, risk tolerance, and budget alignment. Over 800 firms have taken it so far.

Take the Quiz →

That’s your week. Verify your citations, update your Word, and reply if you’ve got a tip.
